title: [skip-ci] Packit/Copr: Fix `podman version` in rpm
body:
Additional rpm patching to show upstream short sha in `podman --version` caused podman-machine-os tests to fail.

This commit gets rid of that patching and instead sets define.gitCommit LDFLAG for Copr rpms.

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name. Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?


LGTM, but I'm no RPM expert

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *lsm5*, *Luap99* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [Luap99,lsm5] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

I would much rather see something else than this - to allow to consume content off an upstream branch and let that commit ID and shortcommit live directly in spec. Note this is how it's done in RHEL/CentOS. There is no need to use packit for this.

> I would much rather see something else than this - to allow to consume content off an upstream branch and let that commit ID and shortcommit live directly in spec. Note this is how it's done in RHEL/CentOS. There is no need to use packit for this.

This was required so that podman-machine-os PRs could fetch rpms directly from packit's copr jobs, pass machine-os' CI tests and directly build machine images before doing an upstream podman release. https://github.com/containers/podman-machine-os/pull/64
